top_signals.title: India Accuses West of Double Standards Over U.S. Russia Oil Sanctions
top_signals.summary: The on-and-off U.S. sanctions on Russian oil and the flipping U.S. position regarding India’s oil purchases from Russia highlight the double standards of the Western nations, Indian Foreign Minister S Jaishankar said on Friday. India turned en masse to Russian oil in 2022, when the U.S. and the EU imposed sanctions on Moscow due to the invasion of Ukraine. Four years later, India is a major buyer of Russia’s crude and Russia is India’s single-largest oil supplier. “At that time, the US specifically asked India to buy Russian…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: China’s Credit Growth Dips as Weak Demand Frustrates Policy Push
top_signals.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed in May from a year earlier, as private demand remained subdued even though regulators pushed banks to lend more.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India Inflation Stays Below Target, Backing RBI Rate Hold
top_signals.summary: India’s inflation came in lower than expected in May, despite high fuel costs, supporting the central bank’s recent decision to keep interest rates unchanged.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Jakarta Plans Indonesia’s First Municipal Bond Within a Year
top_signals.summary: Jakarta aims to issue Indonesia’s first municipal bond within a year as part of efforts to diversify city revenue sources following budget cuts.

top_signals.summary: SpaceX has achieved its goal of becoming the largest IPO on record.
